title: Luxuriate in Discomfort
narrative:
Luxuriate in discomfort is a central philosophy for Lisa Wood and a theme running through her practice — stepping into challenging conditions and staying there long enough for perception to change. It is a studio philosophy carried across three projects: a book, a one-night installation, and a public art project concept to improve teenage mental health.

Luxuriate in Discomfort considers our relationship with discomfort, and how the relationship shapes our experience. Together, the works confront our pursuit of comfort and luxury via a double entendre: by living a life of luxury in day-to-day torment, and by seeking discomfort as a means toward contentment. Born out of a 2.5 year inquiry into the nature of pain, the work asks what is luxury's ultimate prize, is it freedom?
medium: Studio philosophy across a book, a one-night installation, and a public art concept
